item.title: If online streaming existed in the Bronze Age, this gamer girl might have been a global legend.
item.description: That other people did not claim or inherit her sheep bones was surprising to me. Seems to me her friends and family had more honour for her memory than to simply take her earnings. Some links about Assyks or Shagai, little animal bones used for games and other purposes: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shagai https://en.dimashnews.com/kazakh-asyk-and-their-counterparts-in-other-countries/ https://mikkeneko.tumblr.com/post/729203279666561024

item.title: Memes favor the literate
item.description:
What happened to the car? Did they get it cleaned?
Corvid haters are not welcome here.
"I'm Merry *brrrp* Poppins, Quill! And also a pickle!"
Technically correct.
You generally shouldn't listen to what the boss says about unions but this is an exception.
Thrawn is a neat character. "I want to understand and appreciate the many diverse cultures of the galaxy which I'm helping to dominate."
For legal reasons this is a joke. For your own safety, keep an eye on the road.
DS9 is the best trek because it has such a deep bench of characters. Morn doesn't even talk and we all remember him. Other treks are lucky when they have a villain as good as Eddington but DS9 also has Wynn and Dukat.
Captain, I am picking up breakfast readings at twice the normal level.
It's Wednesday somewhere
Worth the risk.
In order: cute animals, cool fantasy scene, me at any given time
This shouldn't really come as a surprise to anyone.
Flesh and Blood TCG tax.

item.title: Sock hair [OC]
item.description:
It only took me 27 years to figure out how to tame my unmanageable hair… I just need a sock! All it took was a flippin’ sock!! So for any of you struggling, I bestow upon you, the knowledge of the hair sock <3 I would add a selfie for reference, but I would likely be sent into orbit for doing so! However if you must see the results, I added an IRL picture to the gram over here: https://www.instagram.com/p/DJm1mCzoPvB/?igsh=Y21uMmE4NjRicm8=
Cat tax! Ms. Beany is lookin’ for snacks! Seems like she’s found one (you <3)
item.dc:creator: AmyRevives

item.title: i stole these memes because they remind me of my book character (dale)
item.description: free ebook at rarust.com bruh, i just today saw my first two reviews, and if i wasn't such a numb bastard i would have cried, man. i really expected people to either hate it or ignore it, and maybe these two were flukes and it really will be hated, but man i'm just so glad someone other than me and my close friends liked it. poured my big dumb heart into this big dumb book, and it's nice that it was worth all the blood, sweat, and tears. <3
item.dc:creator: rarust

item.title: What Happened Today #103
item.description: They shook hands with dictators and sold out America while you were working for a living. Trump lifts sanctions on Syria's al-Qaeda-linked president and pockets a $400 million Qatari jet while Musk cuts Saudi deals and Tesla's board dumps stock. At home? They're purging intelligence officials, downsizing military brass, poisoning your drinking water, and silencing anyone who objects. America is suffering from a hostile takeover where loyalty to autocrats matters more than loyalty to Americans. Don't rely on the algorithm. Let me send this to you every night, free and private, so that you never miss what's going on. Add your email on the homepage of https://www.govbrief.today #GovBriefToday #Resist 1. https://go.govbrief.today/syria-sanctions-saudi 2. https://go.govbrief.today/turkey-ukraine-rubio 3. https://go.govbrief.today/India-disputes-trump 4. https://go.govbrief.today/doge-deletes-claims 5. https://go.govbrief.today/niosh-head-restored 6. https://go.govbrief.today/harvard-grants-450 7. https://go.govbrief.today/climate-pages-restored 8. https://go.govbrief.today/epa-weaken-water-rules 9. https://go.govbrief.today/hegseth-officer-purge 10. https://go.govbrief.today/gabbard-fires-officials 11. https://go.govbrief.today/martin-biden-pardons 12. https://go.govbrief.today/judge-enemies-act-ok 13. https://go.govbrief.today/Milwaukee-judge-indicted 14. https://go.govbrief.today/states-sue-immigration-funding 15. https://go.govbrief.today/emergency-housing-vouchers 16. https://go.govbrief.today/florida-drag-show 17. https://go.govbrief.today/schumer-holds-nominees 18. https://go.govbrief.today/saudi-musk-starlink 19. https://go.govbrief.today/denholm-dumped-tesla 20. https://go.govbrief.today/tesla-fed-answers

item.title: Things seen this week during structural assessments!
item.description:
This foundation is severely deteriorated. This type of deterioration often traces back to older construction practices where some builders used beach sand in their concrete mixes, introducing high levels of salt.
This is a clear example of severe wood rot caused by prolonged moisture exposure. What makes this especially concerning is how easily this kind of damage can go unnoticed until it affects the structure's stability. Early detection is key to avoiding more invasive repairs.
Moisture intrusion is evident with efflorescence forming on this internal stem wall. Not only is this foundation showing signs of water damage, but the brick foundation on the right doesn't meet current building codes. As unreinforced masonry, it is vulnerable to damage during earthquakes, posing a significant safety risk.
In another area of the crawlspace, you can see additional signs of deterioration consisting of rusting steel plates, concrete in poor conditions, and more water damage throughout the foundation.
Plants were growing right next to this foundation and eventually made their way into the crawlspace. This is why it’s important to be mindful when planting trees and vegetation near your home as they can cause structural problems over time.
This foundation has a ton of spall damage. Spalling is the process where concrete begins to break and flake off, typically caused by moisture intrusion.
As the water seeps into the concrete, it can weaken the material, leading to cracks, crumbling, and exposed rebar. This not only affects the foundation's strength but can also result in further structural damage if left unaddressed, due to its likeliness of spreading.
This foundation was likely blown out to accommodate for plumbing or electrical systems to run through it. As a result, there is now no proper support for the home.
Thank you for joining us, we hope you enjoyed! We've noticed that a lot of you ask to show how we repair the damage we find. We just completed a full project walkthrough of this failing retaining wall and how we replaced it. Check it out here if you're interested: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9N8r3ZgCBrk
item.dc:creator: AlphaStructural

item.title: Return of the Meme: 1360 (friendly pelican)
item.description:
Let's get this bread.
Unrestrained and unfettered capitalism kills everything it touches.
Mood. Did some research, and basically, the courier job is just above scam level. I haven't heard back yet about a second interview for the escrow assistant thing. I DO have an interview today for a middle school history teacher position at a local charter school, here's hoping I don't fuck that up.
The Dude Abides.
I absolutely love this contemporary icon of the Resurrection. The vibe here is immaculate.
@TheBloodyLady gave me this point.
All roads lead to Rome.
Awwwwwwwww yesss. This can include non-fiction books with a map, like the one I'm reading now about Assyria.
Also, often someone brings a hookah, cigars, and SOMEONE is smoking a pipe. Say what you want about us, we do know how to party.
Basically, all of them. I don't really have any dreams left.
They are the absolute best women.
Fucking tell me now.
Pippin tax. Today's message from Pippin: "Why thank you! I love you. [Existential Conure screaming]" ______ 1) Are you on the run to a safe place and passing through Arizona on your way to safer grounds? My apartment is open to you to crash at, and it is a safe space. Pippin and I will make you a meal and gladly share tea, wine, or mead with you. 2) Hey! If you're a woman or minority in a STEM field in Arizona living north of Phoenix and you need new headshots for this website or another detailing your experiences/research/accomplishment because our monstrous regime has deleted your experience from an official government website, PM me. Pippin and I have a good Nikon DSLR, and we'll do the photos for free, and, uh, you'll get to meet Pippin, I guess? (If you know someone who could use this who's not on Imgur, feel free to PM me to get my email address for them and tell them they're free to message me.) 3) Take your meds with a full glass of water, please. Do it for Pippin, or Space Mom, or better yet, do it for yourself. 4) Ĝefeloað wē. We will carry on. 5) all funds from the sales of items at these two links will be sent to support charities aiding to fight for Trans rights in the US (not just T-shirts!) https://www.teepublic.com/t-shirt/74812801-all-conures-are-anarchists https://www.teepublic.com/t-shirt/74811778-whiptail-pride

item.title: More dissent : The Episcopal Church’s migration service is refusing a directive from the federal government
item.description: The Episcopal Church’s migration service is refusing a directive from the federal government to help resettle white South Africans granted refugee status, citing the church’s longstanding “commitment to racial justice and reconciliation.” Presiding Bishop Sean Rowe announced the step Monday, shortly before 59 South Africans arrived at Dulles International Airport outside Washington on a private charter plane and were greeted by a government delegation. Episcopal Migration Ministries instead will halt its decades-long partnership with the government, Rowe said. President Donald Trump opened a fast-tracked refugee status to white South Africans, accusing their government of discrimination, even as his administration abruptly shut down the overall U.S. refugee program. The South Africans jumped ahead of thousands of would-be refugees overseas who had been undergoing years of vetting and processing. Episcopal Migration Ministries has long resettled refugees under federal grants. Rowe said that about two weeks ago, the government contacted it and said it expected the ministry to resettle some of the South Africans under terms of its grant. “In light of our church’s steadfast commitment to racial justice and reconciliation and our historic ties with the Anglican Church of Southern Africa, we are not able to take this step,” Rowe said. “Accordingly, we have determined that, by the end of the federal fiscal year, we will conclude our refugee resettlement grant agreements with the U.S. federal government.” Another faith-based group, Church World Service, said it is open to helping resettle the Afrikaners. South Africa’s government has vehemently denied allegations of discriminatory treatment of its white minority residents. “It has been painful to watch one group of refugees, selected in a highly unusual manner, receive preferential treatment over many others who have been waiting in refugee camps or dangerous conditions for years,” Rowe said. “I am saddened and ashamed that many of the refugees who are being denied entrance to the United States are brave people who worked alongside our military in Iraq and Afghanistan and now face danger at home because of their service to our country.” He also said many refugees, including Christians, are victims of religious persecution and are now denied entry. The move marks the end of a ministry-government partnership that, for nearly four decades, has served nearly 110,000 refugees from countries, including Ukraine, Myanmar and Congo, Rowe said. It’s not the first high-profile friction between the Episcopal Church and the Trump administration. Bishop Mariann Budde of Washington drew Trump’s anger in January at an inaugural prayer service in which she urged “mercy” on those fearing his actions, including migrants and LGBTQ+ children. The Anglican Church of Southern Africa includes churches in South Africa and neighboring countries. It was a potent force in the campaign against apartheid in the 1980s and 1990s, an effort for which the late Archbishop Desmond Tutu received the Nobel Peace Prize in 1984.
item.dc:creator: JasonM1
